attestation.statement: Clinician (KL, clinical sign-off authority) ruled in-session on 2026-07-22 that, for the low-acuity telehealth product scope (routine everyday dermatology; the service does NOT accept emergency presentations), this case's baseline tier is recalibrated T3→T2 (prompt GP / telehealth management). The named clinical reds are UNCHANGED and remain escalated: skin malignancy (melanoma / SCC / BCC), spreading or systemic soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot / limb ulcers), autoimmune bullous disease, and eye-risk HSV. The case's escalation edges are retained as the safety net. No autonomous diagnosis; no paediatric dosing.
attestation.supersedes: 2026-07-17 bulk DST clinician attestation (for this case)
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit in-session ruling)
attestation.scope: this case (derm T3→T2 recalibration for low-acuity product scope)

codes_manifest.display: Burn (candidate for chemical burn of scalp)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~42) — no age stated in the source note; a 5-year history of patterned hair loss with early balding in father/brother indicates an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as male from the described M-shaped bitemporal recession / vertex balding and the source note's reference to male-pattern hair loss.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected the scalp, measured vitals and supervised decontamination. Patient/video-observable findings (sharply demarcated weeping erythema, general distress, the product bottle) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician-measured vitals (BP 138/86, HR 95, Temp 37.1, RR 16, SpO2 98%) and the un-gradeable injury depth sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician-supervised scalp wash was reprojected to patient-performed rinsing.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header foregrounds the chronic benign condition (androgenetic alopecia); the scoreable acute diagnosis is the product-induced contact dermatitis. The green side (01/02) carries only the acute presentation plus lay hair-loss background, with no diagnosis label.

Tier set to T3 (narrow telehealth-limit uplift): systemically well with no airway features, but chemical-injury depth cannot be graded remotely — urgent same-day in-person assessment is the correct disposition. Both under-action (routine reassurance) and reflexive 000/ED over-escalation are scored as errors.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DERM-03-00205) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-03 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Differential SNOMED codes (chemical burn 125666000 recorded as a generic 'Burn' candidate; cellulitis, seborrhoeic dermatitis, anaphylaxis) and the androgenetic alopecia code are best-effort candidates pending terminology receipt.

Drug AMT/PBS codes for betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% lotion and loratadine were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.
Doses (betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% lotion applied twice daily; loratadine 10 mg once daily) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
